title: Model Decay: Why Models Grow Old
concept: Model decay is the gradual drop in a deployed model's accuracy over time, because it reflects a snapshot of a world that keeps changing after training ends.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What does F1-Score balance between?

options.text: Precision and Recall, giving one combined score.
The number of clusters and centroids.

exercises.explain: Correct! F1-Score is the harmonic balance of Precision and Recall.

cards.title: The carton of milk in the fridge
cards.body: A carton of milk is perfectly fresh the day you buy it. But leave it in the fridge for a month, and it spoils, even though nothing physically changed inside the sealed carton. A deployed model is similar: it was 'fresh' the day it finished training, but the world outside keeps moving without it.

cards.title: Model Decay: The silent expiration
cards.body: Model decay (or model staleness) is the gradual drop in a model's real-world accuracy over time. The model itself never changes after deployment, but the patterns in the real world it was trained to recognize keep shifting.

cards.title: An outdated road map
cards.body: A model is like a printed road map from five years ago. The roads it shows were accurate on the day it was printed. But new roads have been built and old ones closed since then. The map itself hasn't changed, but it's now giving you wrong directions.

cards.title: A pandemic-era shopping model
cards.body: A model trained in 2019 to predict which products customers buy together learns patterns from pre-pandemic shopping habits. In 2020, buying habits shift overnight (more home goods, less travel gear). The model's old rules no longer match new behavior, so its recommendations get worse.

cards.title: A model isn't broken just because it's decaying
cards.body: You might assume a decaying model has a bug that needs fixing. In reality, the model's code and math are working exactly as designed — the problem is that the real-world patterns it learned from have simply moved on without it.

cards.title: Fresh Model vs. Decayed Model
cards.body: How a model's usefulness changes after deployment:

left.text:
Trained on recent, relevant data.
High accuracy.
Patterns match reality.

right.text:
Trained on old, outdated data.
Accuracy has dropped.
Patterns no longer match reality.

options.text: The gradual drop in a deployed model's accuracy as real-world patterns shift away from its training data.
A bug in the model's source code that must be patched.
The process of compressing a model file to save disk space.
The act of deleting old training data.

exercises.explain: Correct! Model decay describes accuracy dropping as the world changes, not a code bug.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A model's own code and math change automatically after it's deployed to keep up with the real world.
exercises.explain: False! A deployed model's rules are frozen at training time; it does not update itself automatically.

exercises.sentence: A model is like an outdated ___ that no longer reflects newly built roads.
exercises.explain: A model is like a map — accurate the day it's made, but stale as the world changes.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario is a classic example of model decay?

options.text: A loan approval model trained on 2015 salary data starts making poor decisions in 2025 because typical salaries have risen significantly.
A model's file gets corrupted during a server crash.
A developer forgets to import a required library.

exercises.explain: Correct! Salaries rising over a decade is a real-world shift the frozen model can't account for.

exercises.prompt: A music app's recommendation model was trained in 2018. In 2024, it keeps recommending outdated songs and misses new popular artists entirely, even though the code has never been touched. What is happening?

options.text: Model decay: the model's frozen 2018 patterns no longer match 2024's music trends, even though the code itself is unchanged.
The model has a coding bug that needs to be fixed by a developer.
The model's hardware is physically wearing out over time.
The app's servers have run out of storage space.

exercises.explain: Exactly! This is textbook model decay — the code is fine, but the frozen patterns are stale. Next, let's learn the two specific technical ways this staleness can happen!
